feed.title: Apple Faces $38 Billion Risk as India Defends Global Turnover Antitrust Law

feed.title: Apple and Chase announce JPMorgan Chase will become new issuer of Apple Card

feed.title: Breaking the decoding bottleneck: Fast and accurate software decoding for Quantum LDPC codes
feed.summary: IonQ has developed a new Beam Search Decoder for Quantum LDPC codes that significantly improves accuracy and speed for quantum error correction. This software-based decoder, which runs on standard CPUs, challenges the industry assumption that supercomputers are needed for large-scale fault-tolerant quantum computing, particularly for trapped-ion architectures. This innovation allows IonQ to achieve fault-tolerant correction with greater efficiency and lower cost compared to other approaches.

feed.title: Apple CEO Steve Jobs Unveils the First iPhone – This Day in History
authors: The Association of Mature American Citizens
feed.summary: On January 9, 2007, Steve Jobs introduced the first iPhone, a device that revolutionized mobile technology by combining a widescreen iPod, a mobile phone, and an internet communicator. Despite initial skepticism, its touchscreen interface and later the App Store transformed the smartphone market and propelled Apple into one of the world's most valuable companies. The iPhone's debut is considered a pivotal moment in tech history, reshaping digital communication and fostering an entire app economy.

feed.title: Why has Apple Chosen Chase to be its New Card Issuer?

The agreement, valued at an estimated $20 billion in card balances, is expected to transition over approximately 24 months, with Mastercard remaining the payment network. This partnership strengthens Chase's position in co-branded finance and digital transactions, allowing existing Apple Card customers to retain current features while gaining access to Chase's extensive infrastructure.

feed.title: US senators ask Apple and Google to remove X, Grok from app stores (AAPL:NASDAQ)

feed.title: Senator Dumps Apple, Google, Microsoft To End 2025—But Refuses To Sell This Mag 7 Underperformer
feed.summary: Senator Shelley Moore Capito (R-W.Va.) sold shares of Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft in December 2025, according to recent disclosures. Despite divesting from these major Magnificent Seven stocks, Capito chose to retain her Meta Platforms shares, which she bought multiple times throughout 2025. This decision is noteworthy given Meta's second-worst performance among the Magnificent Seven in 2025, suggesting a long-term belief in the company perhaps due to undervaluation.

feed.title: 2026 US Stock Market Outlook: Where to Find Investing Opportunities
feed.summary: The US equity market was trading at a 4% discount to fair value estimates as of December 31, 2025, driven by significant fair value increases in mega-cap stocks and despite an overall market rise. Investors can find attractive opportunities in small-cap, mid-cap, and certain large-cap growth and value stocks, with Real Estate, Technology, Energy, and Communications sectors showing significant undervaluation. The outlook for 2026 predicts continued volatility due to high AI valuations, a change in Fed leadership, trade negotiations, slowing economic growth, and midterm elections, suggesting a barbell-shaped portfolio strategy to balance upside potential with risk management.

feed.title: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
feed.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC (STX) stock recorded a strong performance on Friday, surpassing its competitors in the technology sector. The stock closed at $96.53, marking a 3.10% increase from the previous day, and traded higher than its 52-week average. This positive movement occurred despite a mixed day for the broader market indices, with the Dow and S&P 500 experiencing slight declines.

feed.title: ANTITRUST—9th Cir.: Heart rate app developer’s claims against Apple flatline on appeal (Jan 9, 2026)
feed.summary: The 9th Circuit Court of Appeals upheld the dismissal of antitrust claims brought by a heart rate app developer against Apple. The developer alleged that Apple's policies regarding App Store distribution and Apple Watch compatibility stifled competition. The court found that the developer failed to adequately define the relevant market and demonstrate Apple's monopoly power within it.

feed.title: Inside IMAX's $1.28B record year and the movie slate behind it
feed.summary: IMAX Corporation reported a record-breaking global box office of $1.28 billion in 2025, surpassing its previous high from 2019 by 13% and increasing 40% year-over-year. This achievement marks the highest-grossing year for IMAX in North America, China, and the Rest of World markets, driven by a diverse slate including a significant number of local language films. Looking ahead, IMAX projects further growth in 2026 with a target of $1.4 billion, supported by an anticipated strong lineup of Filmed for IMAX releases.

feed.title: How one Apple-focused security tool just landed a top Gartner ranking
feed.summary: Jamf (NASDAQ: JAMF), a company specializing in managing and securing Apple devices for businesses, has been named a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Endpoint Management Tools. The recognition highlights Jamf's Apple-first platform, which integrates management, security, and AI-driven automation, offering deep Apple-native support and enterprise-grade security. Jamf's approach contrasts with cross-platform tools by providing zero-touch deployment and same-day OS support, enhancing efficiency and user experience for organizations using Apple ecosystems.

feed.title: What Makes IMAX Corporation (IMAX) a 2026 EDM Top Idea
feed.summary: IMAX Corporation (NYSE: IMAX) has been identified as a 2026 Entertainment & Digital Media (EDM) Top Idea by analysts due to its strong global box office share, rising operating leverage, and network expansion. Benchmark Co. analyst Mike Hickey set a Buy rating with a $42 price target, while Wells Fargo analyst Omar Mejias raised his price target to $47, citing the company's diversified business model and ability to capitalize on the demand for premium cinematic experiences.

feed.title: The Smartest Dividend ETF to Buy With $2,000 Right Now
feed.summary: This article identifies the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) as a smart dividend ETF for investors seeking high yields from top technology stocks. The JEPQ ETF utilizes a covered call strategy on Nasdaq-100 companies, resulting in a monthly dividend and a yield exceeding 10%. While it doesn't aim for significant capital gains from stock appreciation, it provides substantial dividend income, with a $2,000 investment potentially yielding nearly $200 annually.

feed.title: Fire Capital Management LLC Grows Position in Apple Inc. $AAPL
feed.summary: Fire Capital Management LLC significantly increased its stake in Apple Inc. ($AAPL) by 86.2% in the third quarter, making it their sixth-largest holding. Despite some insider selling, institutional ownership remains high, and Wall Street analysts generally hold a "Moderate Buy" rating for Apple. The company recently reported an earnings beat and announced a quarterly dividend, while facing mixed sentiment regarding AI progress, regulatory risks, and ongoing iPhone demand.

feed.title: Is Opera (OPRA) Using ESOP Capacity and Conferences to Quietly Recast Its Capital Strategy?
feed.summary: Opera Limited recently filed a US$42.54 million shelf registration for 3,000,000 American Depositary Shares under its employee stock ownership plan (ESOP) and its CFO participated in the Needham Growth Conference. These actions suggest a strategic focus on capital flexibility and deeper institutional engagement, while reaffirming existing dividends and funding product innovation for its AI browser and MiniPay fintech efforts. The article highlights that these developments, along with dependence on third-party AI, raise questions about Opera's long-term earnings profile and capital allocation.

feed.title: SJS Investment Consulting Inc. Acquires 5,192 Shares of JPMorgan Chase & Co. $JPM
feed.summary: SJS Investment Consulting Inc. significantly increased its stake in JPMorgan Chase & Co. by 260.8% in Q3, adding 5,192 shares to now hold 7,183 shares valued at approximately $2.266 million. JPMorgan recently declared a quarterly dividend of $1.50 per share and is set to become the new issuer of the Apple Card, though this transition is expected to incur a $2.2 billion provision that could impact Q4 earnings. Analysts currently have a mixed outlook on JPM, with a consensus target price of $333.90.

feed.title: WESPAC Advisors Increased Its Position in First Trust NASDAQ Clean Edge Smart Grid Infrastructure. Is the Index Fund a Buy?
feed.summary: WESPAC Advisors SoCal, LLC significantly increased its stake in the First Trust NASDAQ Clean Edge Smart Grid Infrastructure Index Fund (NASDAQ:GRID) by acquiring 32,351 shares, valuing the transaction at approximately $4.96 million. This move more than doubled their previous holdings, suggesting a bullish outlook on the fund, likely driven by the rising electricity demands of artificial intelligence and the push towards clean energy. The GRID fund focuses on companies involved in electric grid modernization and smart energy management, with a strong one-year return of 30.1%.

feed.title: NZAC vs. URTH: How A Climate-Focused ETF Matches Up With An International Powerhouse
feed.summary: This article compares two ETFs, NZAC (SPDR MSCI ACWI Climate Paris Aligned ETF) and URTH (iShares MSCI World ETF), highlighting their differences in focus, cost, performance, and holdings. While URTH offers broader developed market exposure, NZAC provides a more cost-effective, climate-focused option aligned with the Paris Agreement. Both funds hold top tech companies globally, but NZAC's stricter ESG screening results in fewer holdings compared to URTH.

feed.title: The ETFs I’d Buy If I Was Starting Over in 2026
feed.summary: The article identifies three Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) as ideal investments for someone starting over in 2026, emphasizing their benefits of low risk, diversification, and growth potential. The recommended ETFs are Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF (VIG), and Invesco NASDAQ 100 ETF (QQQM), each offering exposure to various sectors and a strong track record. These funds are highlighted for their ability to navigate market fluctuations and provide steady returns and income, making them suitable for long-term portfolio building.

feed.title: Apple Inc. $AAPL Stock Position Lessened by Valley National Advisers Inc.
feed.summary: Valley National Advisers Inc. has reduced its stake in Apple Inc. (AAPL) by 13.0% in Q3, now holding 96,086 shares valued at $24.47 million, making it their fourth-largest holding. Despite this reduction and recent insider selling, analyst sentiment for Apple remains largely positive, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $284.07, driven by strong iPhone 17 demand and strategic partnerships. The article also notes mixed sentiment regarding AI readiness and regulatory risks for Apple's app store.

feed.title: Jim Cramer on Microsoft: "Stock's Been Punished By the Fact That Management Wants to Spend a Fortune on AI"
feed.summary: Jim Cramer discussed Microsoft's stock performance, noting its downward trend despite strong company performance. He attributed this to management's significant AI spending and concerns surrounding OpenAI's financial stability, in which Microsoft holds a substantial stake. Cramer remains optimistic about Microsoft, especially if OpenAI can secure funding at a high valuation and if Azure's growth exceeds recently lowered guidance.

feed.title: Apple Reaffirmed as Evercore's Top Pick on iPhone Strength
feed.summary: Evercore ISI has reaffirmed Apple Inc. (AAPL) as its top technology hardware pick, citing continued strength in iPhone sales and the anticipated positive impact of AI-driven updates to Siri. The firm expects Apple to exceed near-term expectations due to a strong product cycle and growing consumer demand, alongside its developing AI capabilities. Evercore also holds positive outlooks for Dell and HP Enterprise, highlighting their respective strengths in supply chain management and positioning for the memory cycle.

feed.title: Latest Legal News | Today's Breaking Headlines
feed.summary: This Reuters article provides a roundup of recent legal news, primarily focusing on cases and decisions impacting U.S. politics and business. Key stories include a federal judge blocking the Trump administration's attempt to end legal status for thousands of Latin American migrants, several states suing the administration over frozen childcare funds, and legal challenges involving AI, trademarks, and corporate mergers. The piece also touches on upcoming legal industry developments and Supreme Court cases.

feed.title: Nvidia resets the economics of AI factories, again
feed.summary: Nvidia's recent announcements at CES 2026, particularly the new Vera Rubin platform, have significantly reset the economics of AI factories by introducing extreme co-design across its entire system architecture. The company is driving annual performance improvements of five times and throughput of ten times, shifting the focus from individual chip performance to system-level efficiency, specifically tokens per rack. This comprehensive redesign solidifies Nvidia's dominant position, widening the gap with competitors and forcing ecosystem players to align with this new paradigm where historical benchmarks like Moore's Law are accelerated tenfold.

feed.title: Revolve group’s Mente Michael sells $6m in shares By Investing.com
feed.summary: Michael Mente, Co-Chief Executive Officer and director at Revolve Group, Inc., sold 194,196 shares of Class A Common Stock for approximately $6.03 million between January 7 and January 9, 2026. These sales were executed through a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan and coincided with the conversion of Class B shares to Class A. Despite the sales, Revolve Group has shown a 48% price increase over the last six months and maintains a "GOOD" financial health score, with several analysts providing positive outlooks and increased price targets.

feed.title: Riding the AI Wave: The iShares MSCI World ETF’s Strong Start to 2026
feed.summary: The iShares MSCI World ETF (URTH) is having a strong start to 2026, driven by a rebound in U.S. tech stocks and the ongoing artificial intelligence trend, with its benchmark MSCI World Index reaching 4,511 points. Despite its impressive performance and liquidity, the ETF carries elevated valuations for many of its top holdings, notably NVIDIA, Apple, and Microsoft, and is strongly influenced by U.S.-led AI dynamism and the U.S. dollar's strength. Investors should monitor valuation risks, the potential for a "Physical AI" rotation, and key technical levels and currency exposure in Q1 2026.

feed.title: 5T Wealth LLC Buys 3,096 Shares of Apple Inc. $AAPL

The article also touches on Apple's recent strong earnings, dividend, analyst ratings, and various positive and negative sentiments affecting its stock performance.

feed.title: Fruth Investment Management Has $6.03 Million Stake in Apple Inc. $AAPL

feed.title: Financiere des Professionnels Fonds d investissement inc. Has $7.99 Million Holdings in JPMorgan Chase & Co. $JPM

This move comes amidst JPMorgan's positive strategic developments, including becoming the new issuer of the Apple Card, which is expected to add substantial consumer-credit growth but will also incur a $2.2 billion provision impacting Q4 earnings. Institutional ownership in JPM remains high at 71.55%, yet analyst consensus leans towards a "Hold" rating despite price target increases from some firms.

feed.title: Intel Sets Sights on Dominating AI PC Market with Ambitious 100M Target, Partners with Microsoft
feed.summary: Intel aims to integrate AI into 100 million PCs by 2025, targeting over 20% of the global PC market. The company plans to ship 40 million AI-enabled PCs this year and 60 million next, collaborating with Microsoft on the AI PC concept featuring Intel's Core Ultra chipset and Microsoft's Copilot. Intel is also seeking over $10 billion in U.S. Chips Act funding and $2 billion in equity for a fabrication plant in Ireland, while facing competition from Apple and Qualcomm.

feed.title: Oregon Public Employees Retirement Fund Increases Stock Position in Electronic Arts Inc. $EA
feed.summary: The Oregon Public Employees Retirement Fund has increased its stake in Electronic Arts Inc. (NASDAQ:EA) by 30.7%, acquiring an additional 6,033 shares to hold a total of 25,692 shares valued at approximately $5.18 million. Despite this increase in institutional holdings, EA recently missed quarterly earnings expectations and saw its revenue decline year-over-year. The company's stock trades near its 52-week high, while analysts maintain a consensus "Hold" rating with an average price target of $181.21.

feed.title: Oregon Public Employees Retirement Fund Acquires 28,146 Shares of Exelon Corporation $EXC
feed.summary: The Oregon Public Employees Retirement Fund increased its stake in Exelon Corporation by 32.2%, acquiring an additional 28,146 shares, bringing their total holdings to 115,447 shares valued at $5.20 million. This move is part of a broader trend of institutional investors significantly increasing their positions in Exelon, with institutional ownership now standing at approximately 80.92%. The article also discusses recent analyst ratings, earnings estimates, and stock performance data for Exelon.

feed.title: Alphabet (GOOG) stock week ahead: Washington pressure hits Google Play as earnings loom
feed.summary: Alphabet (GOOG) stock faces pressure as Democratic Senators demand Google and Apple remove X and Grok from their app stores due to policy violations, just as the company prepares for its upcoming earnings report on February 4th. Investors are also watching the EU antitrust regulators' decision by February 10th regarding Alphabet's $32 billion acquisition of Wiz, which is crucial for Google Cloud. The week ahead will also see the release of the U.S. Consumer Price Index for December 2025, which could impact interest rate expectations and high-multiple tech stocks like GOOG.

feed.title: HK Golden embarks on Nasdaq IPO journey with TROOPS backing By Investing.com
feed.summary: HK Golden, Inc., a prominent Hong Kong online forum and strategic partner of TROOPS, Inc., is preparing for a Nasdaq IPO targeted for 2026. The company plans to launch a proprietary token ecosystem to reward user engagement, which can be used for advertising or insurance products within the TROOPS network. TROOPS, despite not currently being profitable, has seen significant stock momentum and maintains a strong financial position, viewing HK Golden's potential listing as a strategic development.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway stock slips into Monday: BRK.B holders brace for CPI and bank earnings
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway's Class B shares (BRK.B) slipped 0.13% to $499.10 on Friday, with Class A shares also dipping, as investors eye upcoming inflation data and bank earnings. With new CEO Greg Abel at the helm, the company faces scrutiny amid shifts in market sentiment towards growth stocks and questions about deploying its significant cash reserves. The article highlights the forthcoming CPI report on Tuesday and major bank earnings as key immediate triggers for market movement.

feed.title: GPIQ ETF: 9.8% Yield At $52.89 – Nasdaq-100 Income Play For The 2026 Tech Rally
feed.summary: The GPIQ ETF, currently trading at $52.89 with a 9.8% yield, is positioned as a high-income Nasdaq-100 play for the anticipated 2026 tech rally. It utilizes an actively managed buy-write strategy on Nasdaq-100 derivatives, offering a more tax-efficient yield and higher upside participation than JEPQ, making it attractive for investors seeking cash flow from tech investments. The fund's risk/reward profile is considered constructive, assuming continued strength in large-cap tech and supportive macroeconomic conditions.

feed.title: IWY vs. VUG: How Fees and Diversification Set These Popular Growth ETFs Apart
feed.summary: This article compares two popular growth ETFs, IWY and VUG, highlighting their differences in expense ratios, diversification, and portfolio construction. While both aim to provide exposure to large-cap U.S. growth stocks, VUG offers a lower expense ratio and holds more stocks, making it a more affordable and potentially more diversified option for investors. IWY, on the other hand, has a slightly higher concentration in its top holdings, particularly in technology.

feed.title: Should Fidelity Nasdaq Composite Index ETF (ONEQ) be on your investing radar?
feed.summary: The Fidelity Nasdaq Composite Index ETF (ONEQ) is a passively managed fund tracking the Nasdaq Composite Index, offering exposure to Large Cap Growth stocks. With $9.54 billion in assets, an expense ratio of 0.21%, and a "Strong Buy" Zacks ETF Rank, it's considered a good option for investors seeking diversified growth exposure, particularly in the information technology sector. The article highlights its performance, risk profile, and comparison to alternative ETFs like VUG and QQQ.

feed.title: AAPL - Apple Inc. (AAPL) — Durable Compounder With Services-Led Earnings Upside
feed.summary: This article analyzes Apple Inc. (AAPL) as a durable compounder, highlighted by its services-led earnings upside despite mature hardware markets. The author's valuation suggests Apple trades near fair value, emphasizing the strength of its integrated ecosystem, pricing power, and aggressive capital allocation strategy. Key growth drivers are identified as high-margin Services, premium pricing for iPhones, and share repurchases, while regulatory pressures and China demand pose risks.

feed.title: Concentrix (CNXC) Q4 Earnings Report Preview: What To Look For
feed.summary: Concentrix (CNXC) is set to report its Q4 earnings this Tuesday, with analysts expecting a 3.6% revenue growth year-on-year to $2.53 billion and adjusted earnings of $2.91 per share. The company has a history of surpassing revenue estimates, and its stock has seen positive investor sentiment, outperforming peers in recent months. Investors will be looking for insights into how the professional services sector is performing, as Concentrix is among the first to report this earnings season.

feed.title: Dow, S&P 500 Notch Record Closes After Market Rebound
feed.summary: The Dow and S&P 500 achieved record closes despite market jitters stemming from a criminal investigation into Fed Chair Jerome Powell. This rebound occurred after an initial deficit for the Dow and healthy gains for the Nasdaq. Key events included bank struggles due to President Trump's call for a credit card rate cap and Alphabet hitting a $4 trillion market cap following an AI partnership with Apple.

feed.title: Apple To Use Google’s Gemini AI For Siri Overhaul: Report
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has reportedly confirmed a multi-year collaboration with Alphabet Inc.'s Google to enhance its artificial intelligence capabilities, including an overhaul of its Siri voice assistant in late 2026. This partnership will leverage Google's cloud services and Gemini AI models as the foundation for Apple's future AI systems. Apple stated that Google's technology provides the most capable foundation for its foundational AI models, indicating a strategic shift towards external AI infrastructure.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Says Goldman Sachs (GS) is a Major Position In His Trust
feed.summary: Jim Cramer has stated that Goldman Sachs (GS) is the largest position in his trust, attributing its strong performance to price appreciation. He noted the bank's undervaluation during the Biden era despite its growth potential, particularly in M&A activity and IPOs. Cramer believes the market misjudged Goldman Sachs's unique franchise and praised CEO David Solomon's efforts to return the firm to its core strengths.

feed.title: Annex Advisory Services LLC Cuts Holdings in Apple Inc. $AAPL
feed.summary: Annex Advisory Services LLC decreased its stake in Apple Inc. by 2.9% in the third quarter, while several other institutional investors significantly increased their holdings. Two insiders, CFO Kevan Parekh and Chris Kondo, also sold a substantial number of shares, reducing their ownership. Despite these sales, analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target price of $284.07, and Apple recently announced a quarterly dividend.
